tagged_story_for_tts:
"Earth’s tilt shifts.<[silence:50ms]> Poles trace circles.<[silence:50ms]>
The wobble, called axial precession, comes from gravity’s tug between Earth’s bulge and the Sun and Moon.<[silence:50ms]>
Like a spinning top slowing down, Earth’s axis draws a cone over 26000 years, altering star positions.<[silence:50ms]>
This changes when spring begins and how sunlight hits the planet.<[silence:50ms]>
Three related subjects are polar drift, Milankovitch cycles and celestial mechanics."
clean_story:
"Earth’s tilt shifts. Poles trace circles.
The wobble, called axial precession, comes from gravity’s tug between Earth’s bulge and the Sun and Moon.
Like a spinning top slowing down, Earth’s axis draws a cone over 26000 years, altering star positions.
This changes when spring begins and how sunlight hits the planet.
Three related subjects are polar drift, Milankovitch cycles and celestial mechanics."
